Request a Demo

What We Monitor

What SAM4 Monitors

Every asset type and failure mode SAM4 detects, and how we detect it.

All monitored from the electrical cabinet. No sensors on the asset required.

Why one technology covers them all

Traditional monitoring requires different sensor types for different assets. ESA works the same way across all AC motor-driven equipment.

One installation point
SAM4 connects inside the motor control cabinet. One location covers the motor and everything it drives.
Works across AC motor-driven assets
Pumps, fans, compressors, conveyors, mixers, aerators. SAM4 monitors any asset driven by an AC induction motor. No asset-specific sensors required.
Detect mechanical faults electrically
Bearing wear, imbalance, misalignment, and cavitation all leave signatures in the electrical supply. SAM4 reads them.

Asset types we monitor

SAM4 covers the full range of rotating equipment in industrial plants. Here are the asset types we see most across our 10,000+ monitored assets.

Centrifugal pumps
Submersible, booster, borehole, process, injection, transfer, and circulation pumps. The most monitored asset type in our fleet.
Common detections Bearing degradation, cavitation, blockage, seal wear, imbalance, efficiency loss
Pump monitoring details →
Fans & blowers
Ventilation fans, cooling fans, process blowers, and aerators. Critical for safe atmospheric conditions in enclosed spaces.
Common detections Imbalance, bearing degradation, belt issues, blade fouling, process deviations
Fan monitoring details →
Conveyors
Belt conveyors, screw conveyors, and chain conveyors. Common in materials handling across mining, pulp, and manufacturing.
Common detections Bearing degradation, misalignment, belt/chain issues, blockage, overload
Conveyor monitoring details →
Low voltage motors
AC induction motors up to 1 kV: the backbone of industrial operations. SAM4 monitors the motor and the entire connected drivetrain.
Common detections Winding faults, bearing degradation, eccentricity, misalignment, supply issues
LV motor monitoring details →
Medium voltage motors
AC motors from 1 kV to 15 kV driving critical process equipment. High-value assets that are expensive to replace and hard to monitor with sensors.
Common detections Winding insulation degradation, rotor bar faults, partial discharge, bearing wear
MV motor monitoring details →
Transmissions
Gearboxes, couplings, belt drives, and chain drives. SAM4 detects faults across the full mechanical transmission path from motor to driven equipment.
Common detections Gear wear, belt/chain degradation, coupling faults, misalignment
Transmission monitoring details →
Compressors
Gas, air, and process compressors, including assets in ATEX/hazardous zones. High-value assets that are expensive to replace.
Common detections Bearing wear, valve faults, imbalance, efficiency degradation
Compressor monitoring details →
Agitators & mixers
Chemical reactors, blending vessels, and mixing tanks. Often submerged or enclosed, making visual inspection impossible.
Common detections Bearing degradation, seal failure, imbalance from product buildup, gearbox issues
Mixer monitoring details →
Electrical submersible pumps
Downhole ESPs in oil & gas production. Operate thousands of metres underground. Pulling the string costs days and hundreds of thousands of dollars.
Common detections Motor degradation, pump wear, gas lock
ESP monitoring details →
Canned motor pumps
Sealless pumps where the motor rotor is immersed in the process fluid. Impossible to access with vibration sensors.
Common detections Bearing degradation, rotor eccentricity, recirculation changes, winding faults
Canned pump monitoring details →
Archimedes screws
Screw pumps used in water treatment inlet works and low-head pumping stations. Critical for preventing pollution events.
Common detections Bearing degradation, trough wear, drive chain faults, efficiency loss
Screw pump monitoring details →
Asset not listed?
SAM4 monitors any AC motor-driven rotating asset. If it draws current, we can likely monitor it. Share your asset list and we'll confirm coverage.
Talk to us →

Find by monitoring challenge

Not sure which asset page to start with? Find the challenge that matches your plant.

Submerged & sealed assets
No physical access for sensors. ESA monitors from the MCC. No contact with the asset required.
Centrifugal pumps → Electrical submersible pumps → Canned motor pumps →
Hazardous & ATEX zones
Explosive atmospheres make sensor installation expensive and restricted. ESA installs in the safe-area MCC.
Compressors → Agitators & mixers →
Remote & unmanned sites
No staff on-site to walk routes. SAM4 monitors continuously and alerts remotely via cellular connection.
Archimedes screws → Conveyors →
The unmonitored fleet
Too many assets, too little budget. ESA scales to hundreds of assets at a fraction of vibration monitoring cost.
Low voltage motors → Fans & blowers →
Complex drivetrains
Motor, gearbox, coupling, driven equipment: vibration sees one point. ESA reads the full transmission path from the current signature.
Transmissions → Compressors →

Where SAM4 applies

SAM4 monitors
  • AC induction motors (asynchronous and synchronous >2 pole pairs)
  • Low voltage (<690V) and medium/high voltage (>690V)
  • Direct online, soft starter, and VFD-driven motors
  • Motors from 1 kW upward
  • Motors operating at as little as 5% of rated load
Current scope boundaries
  • DC motors and servo motors are not supported
  • 2-pole synchronous motors and generators require technical assessment
  • Assets operating less than 10 hours per month lack sufficient data for reliable baselines
  • Duty cycles under 5 minutes provide insufficient signal for analysis
  • Complex drivetrains with many rotating components may need case-by-case assessment
  • For VFD-driven motors, SAM4 detects faults between the drive and the motor. Power quality issues upstream of the VFD are not visible.
  • Partial discharge detection is not available for medium-voltage assets

What we detect

ESA detects both electrical and mechanical faults through the motor current signature. One measurement point covers the full drive train.

SAM4 detects these failure modes across asset types by reading changes in the electrical signature. Each row shows what fails, how it shows up electrically, and which assets are affected.

Failure mode Component Detection type Typical lead time Affected assets
Bearing degradation Bearing (motor or driven) Mechanical Weeks to months Pumps, Fans, Conveyors, LV motors, MV motors
Impeller wear / cavitation Pump hydraulics Process Days to weeks Pumps, ESPs
Misalignment Coupling / shaft Mechanical Weeks Pumps, Fans, Compressors, Transmissions
Stator winding insulation Motor electrical Electrical Weeks to months LV motors, MV motors, ESPs
Rotor bar defects Motor electrical Electrical Months LV motors, MV motors
Belt / chain degradation Power transmission Mechanical Days to weeks Fans, Transmissions, Conveyors
Gearbox wear Gearbox Mechanical Weeks to months Transmissions, Mixers, Conveyors
Dry running / seal failure Pump process Process Hours to days Pumps, Canned motor pumps
Clogging / blockage Pump / conveyor process Process Hours to days Pumps, Conveyors, Archimedes screws
Supply voltage imbalance Power supply Electrical Immediate LV motors, MV motors

Detection performance at scale

90.8%
Detection accuracy
0.32%
False communication rate
10,000+
Assets monitored
80+
Customers worldwide

0.32% false communication rate. Every alert goes through engineer validation before reaching your team. When SAM4 raises an alert, it means something. Your maintenance team trusts it because false alarms are nearly zero.

How we measure this

Detection rates calculated from 1,874 resolved incidents across 10,000+ monitored assets. "Core three" covers pumps, fans, and conveyors. False communication rate measures alerts not confirmed as genuine anomalies. Period: January 2024 to February 2026. Methodology: SAM4 alert issued, field verification by customer or Samotics service engineer, outcome logged.

How ESA compares to vibration monitoring →

System works very well, support is also very good which is fundamental in making further improvements. Critical in our monitoring of hard to reach assets such as submersible pumps.

Michael Horner, Yorkshire Water

Normally we would have called technicians either at night or in the weekend to make sure that we were able to continue running. And on an emergency the cost would be way higher than being able to plan it.

Jac Valkenburg, Project Lead, Nyrstar

What ESA can and cannot detect

Motor requirements, operating conditions, signal constraints, and the faults that fall outside ESA's detection envelope.

See detection boundaries →

Find out what SAM4 can detect on your assets

Share your asset list. We'll show you which failure modes ESA covers and what detection lead times to expect.

Talk to an engineer

Monitoring the assets your sensors can't reach.
